meaning: n.(天主教的)洗足礼,<天主教>濯足仪式时分发的救济金
examples: Hence today is called Maundy Thursday, or literally "Commandment" Thursday.
It was the year the Queen came to Armagh for a Royal Maundy Thursday service.
Pope Francis has washed the feet of prisoners in a youth detention centre near Rome as part of the Maundy Thursday service.
Every year the Queen visits a cathedral to make the presentation on Maundy Thursday in a ceremony that dates back to the 13th century.
